title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: IFCB data formats supported? : app reads standard IFCB files: ROI files (.roi) - Image data ADC files (.adc) - Metadata (dimensions, timestamps) HDR files (.hdr) - Header information Q: Can use non-IFCB data? : app specifically designed IFCB data format. imaging systems, need adapt data loading functions. Q: data modified? : . app reads original files. output written separate folders. Q: can review images specific class? : Use Class Review mode. Switch “Class Review” using mode toggle sidebar, select Database source, select class dropdown, click Load. loads images annotated class every sample database. can reclassify mistakes using normal relabeling tools. Q: Can reclassify images across multiple samples ? : Yes. Class Review mode (Database source) loads images samples saves changes row-level updates database. means images reclassify updated — annotations samples remain untouched. Q: Can sort folder PNG images class subfolders? : Yes. Class Review mode, select External PNG Folder source, browse PNG folder, click Load Folder. images loaded initial class label (defaulting folder name). Relabel images gallery, set export folder click Export Split Folders copy images class-name subfolders. works independently database. Q: Can classify images without pre-computed classifier files? : Yes. Configure Gradio API URL model Settings > Live Prediction, click Predict button sidebar loading sample. sends images remote CNN model applies predictions directly. See User Guide details.

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: see “Python available” warning : warning appears storage format includes .mat files. Python needed default SQLite storage. see warning don’t need .mat files, switch SQLite Settings > Annotation Storage. Otherwise, enable .mat support: restart app. Q: need Python use ClassiPyR? : . default storage format SQLite, works box Python dependency. Python needed want export .mat files ifcb-analysis compatibility. Q: Python virtual environment created? : default, ifcb_py_install() creates virtual environment ~/.virtualenvs/iRfcb. can specify different location: can also specify venv path launching app: Q: Python virtual environment path resolved? : app uses following priority order: venv_path argument passed run_app() (highest priority) Saved settings previous session (stored settings.json) Default ./venv working directory specify run_app(venv_path = "/path//venv"), path used Python initialization pre-filled Settings dialog, overriding previously saved path. Q: Package installation fails : Make sure remotes installed try: Q: “find app directory” error : Try reinstalling package: Q: iRfcb won’t install : iRfcb core dependency ClassiPyR installed automatically. encounter issues:
code: library(iRfcb) ifcb_py_install()  # Creates venv in current working directory ifcb_py_install("/path/to/your/venv") run_app(venv_path = "/path/to/your/venv") if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("EuropeanIFCBGroup/ClassiPyR") remotes::install_github("EuropeanIFCBGroup/ClassiPyR") install.packages("iRfcb")

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: samples appear dropdown : Check : ROI/PNG Data Folder points data Either ROI files exist, extracted PNG sample folders exist (example .../D20230313T004021_IFCB134/D20230313T004021_IFCB134_00002.png) Click Sync button (circular arrow icon) rescan folders recently added new data Q: “ROI file found” error : app scans ROI/PNG Data Folder recursively, subfolder layout works (including flat). Check : ROI/PNG Data Folder path correct using ROI files, .roi file matching .adc file directory using extracted PNGs, sample folders named DYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS_IFCBNNN image files follow sample_#####.png Click Sync button rescan recently moved added files Q: Classifications loading : CSV files: Must columns named file_name class_name (exact names required) Optionally include score class_name_auto columns CSV file must named sample (e.g., D20230101T120000_IFCB134.csv) File Classification Folder (indexed via file cache; click Sync refresh) H5 files: Must match pattern *_class*.h5 Requires hdf5r package (install.packages("hdf5r")) MAT files: Must match pattern *_class*.mat Must contain roinum TBclass variables

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: classification CSV look like? : CSV must columns named file_name class_name. file must named sample (e.g., D20230101T120000_IFCB134.csv). Minimal example: optional score class_name_auto columns: class_name_auto column contains raw prediction without threshold. “Apply classification threshold” disabled Settings, ClassiPyR uses class_name_auto instead class_name. Q: CNN classifier outputs different column names : column names must exactly file_name class_name. classifier uses different names, rename columns loading. example R:
code: file_name,class_name D20230101T120000_IFCB134_00001.png,Diatom D20230101T120000_IFCB134_00002.png,Ciliate file_name,class_name,class_name_auto,score D20230101T120000_IFCB134_00001.png,unclassified,Diatom,0.45 D20230101T120000_IFCB134_00002.png,Ciliate,Ciliate,0.87 D20230101T120000_IFCB134_00003.png,Dinoflagellate,Dinoflagellate,0.72 df <- read.csv("my_classifications.csv") names(df)[names(df) == "predicted_class"] <- "class_name" names(df)[names(df) == "filename"] <- "file_name" write.csv(df, "D20230101T120000_IFCB134.csv", row.names = FALSE)

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: need upload class list file start annotating? : ! can create class list scratch directly app: Open Settings → Edit Class List (file upload needed) Add classes one time paste multiple classes text area Click Apply Changes - temporary file created automatically Start annotating immediately SQLite storage (default), class list auto-saved database restored next startup. can still export .mat .txt sharing backup. Q: create class list scratch? : Open Settings → Edit Class List Add classes using “Add new class” field, type/paste classes text area Click Apply Changes SQLite storage, class list persists automatically. Optionally save .mat .txt portability. Q: class list saved automatically? : Yes, using SQLite storage (default). Every change — adding classes, renaming, applying WoRMS matches, uploading file — auto-saved global_class_list table SQLite database. next startup, class list restored database. don’t use SQLite storage, save class list .txt .mat file preserve . Q: Can import class list MATLAB? : Yes, load existing class2use.mat file via Settings. Q: class names look different loading : app may truncate trailing numbers class names (e.g., “Diatom_01” → “Diatom”). matches iRfcb behavior. Q: characters class names changed? : Class names used folder names exporting PNGs, certain characters must sanitized: Example: Snowella/Woronichinia becomes Snowella_Woronichinia Common taxonomic characters like hyphens (-), underscores (_), periods (.), spaces preserved. Q: match class names WoRMS AphiaID values? : Settings → Edit Class List, click Match WoRMS AphiaID. app sanitizes class names querying WoRMS Long names (>80 characters) skipped automatically auto-match Unmatched/skipped classes can rematched manually editing query fields clicking Rematch Unmatched Click Apply AphiaID Matches persist results Matched AphiaIDs shown class list [AphiaID: ...]. Q: AphiaID mappings stored? : SQLite database (annotations.sqlite), table class_taxonomy, configured Database Folder. embedded class2use.mat/.txt files.

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: annotations saved? : Annotations split across two locations: SQLite database (default): stored Database Folder (local directory) MAT files statistics: stored Output Folder (can network drive) default, database stored persistent local directory (tools::R_user_dir("ClassiPyR", "data")). Back annotations.sqlite preserve work. Q: default database location? : default Database Folder platform-specific local directory: Linux: ~/.local/share/R/ClassiPyR/ macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/org.R-project.R/R/ClassiPyR/ Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%/R/data/R/ClassiPyR/ can find exact path : can change Settings > Database Folder, always local drive. Q: Can put database network drive? : . SQLite databases safe network filesystems (NFS, SMB/CIFS) network file locking unreliable, can lead database corruption. Always keep Database Folder local drive. Output Folder (MAT files statistics) can safely network drive. Q: transfer annotations another computer? : Since SQLite database stored locally, simply share network drive. Instead, use .mat files interchange format: Export source computer (requires Python scipy): use Export SQLite → .mat button Settings. Import target computer: use Import .mat → SQLite button Settings. Already-imported samples skipped automatically. can also simply copy annotations.sqlite file directly machines prefer. Q: Can import annotations back MATLAB? : Yes, save “MAT file” “” storage format, MAT files compatible ifcb-analysis toolbox (Sosik & Olson, 2007). Use list startMC, load list MATLAB using: Note: Python scipy must installed save .mat files. Change storage format Settings > Annotation Storage. Q: Can migrate existing .mat annotations SQLite database? : Yes. easiest way Import .mat → SQLite button Settings > Annotation Storage, bulk-imports .mat files output folder. can also import programmatically — single file: bulk-import .mat files folder: Q: Can export SQLite annotations back .mat files? : Yes. Use Export SQLite → .mat button Settings > Annotation Storage export annotated samples . requires Python scipy. can also export programmatically: Q: Can create MATLAB-format ZIP archive sharing? : Yes. Use Export SQLite → MATLAB ZIP button Settings > Export SQLite. bundles .mat annotation files, feature CSVs, class2use.mat config file, optional raw data, README files distributable ZIP archive via iRfcb::ifcb_zip_matlab(). need provide features folder; data folder raw IFCB files optional. storage format SQLite-, annotations automatically converted temporary .mat files (requires Python scipy). See iRfcb image export tutorial details archive format. Q: Can import images classified another tool? : Yes. Organize PNG images subfolders named class (e.g., Diatom/, Ciliate_002/). use Import PNG → SQLite Settings > Import / Export. app strips trailing _NNN suffixes folder names (following iRfcb convention) maps images class names based subfolder . folder class names don’t match app’s current class list, mapping dialog appear letting remap existing classes add new classes. Note: ROI files needed viewing images re-exporting. Without ROI files, annotations stored database images displayed gallery. can also import programmatically: Q: Can change annotator name existing annotations? : Yes. Use update_annotator() R console: function returns named vector showing many annotation rows updated per sample (0 means sample found database). Q: ’s statistics CSV? : Two files per sample: *_validation_stats.csv - Summary statistics *_validation_detailed.csv - Per-image details

Settings stored configuration file: Linux: ~/.config/R/ClassiPyR/settings.json macOS: ~/Library/Preferences/org.R-project.R/R/ClassiPyR/settings.json Windows: %APPDATA%/R/config/R/ClassiPyR/settings.json Folder paths, class list location, Python venv path automatically restored restart app. Q: reset settings defaults? : Use reset_settings argument launching app: deletes saved settings.json file starts app default values. folder paths, class list reference, Python venv path cleared, need reconfigure . class list file (class2use_saved.*) deleted config directory loaded re-upload . useful : app fails start due invalid saved paths Folder paths point locations longer exist want clean slate changing data layout can also combine arguments: Q: ’s yellow warning classes? : Classes marked warning classification data class list. can happen : Loading classifications classifier trained different class list class list modified since classification created can still work classes, consider adding class list consistency. Q: Can switch annotation mode sample auto-classifications? : Yes. sample auto-classification data (✓ ✎✓) shows mode toggle header. title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: Q: file index cache? : file index cache stores locations IFCB files (ROI, classification, annotation) found configured folders. ’s saved disk app doesn’t need re-scan entire folder hierarchy every time starts. significantly speeds startup large datasets. Q: refresh file cache? : Click Sync button (circular arrow icon) sidebar, next sample navigation buttons. cache age indicator shows last scan occurred. Q: New samples added aren’t showing : app loads cached file index. Click Sync button rescan folders pick new files. Q: Can update cache without opening app? : Yes. Use rescan_file_index() R console scheduled script: reads folder paths saved settings rebuilds cache. can also pass paths explicitly: Q: cache file stored? : config directory settings: Linux: ~/.config/R/ClassiPyR/file_index.json macOS: ~/Library/Preferences/org.R-project.R/R/ClassiPyR/file_index.json Windows: %APPDATA%/R/config/R/ClassiPyR/file_index.json
code: ClassiPyR::rescan_file_index() ClassiPyR::rescan_file_index(   roi_folder = "/data/ifcb/raw",   csv_folder = "/data/ifcb/classified",   output_folder = "/data/ifcb/manual" )

title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
text: File index cache - app caches folder scan results fast startup. Click Sync ’ve added new data. Use pagination - Lower images per page faster loading Filter class - Reduces rendering load Close apps - Image extraction uses memory SSD storage - Faster file access Scheduled rescans - servers regularly arriving data, use ClassiPyR::rescan_file_index() cron job keep cache current without manual intervention Dashboard: specify dataset - Large Dashboard instances (e.g., habon-ifcb.whoi.edu 900,000+ samples) slow load without dataset filter. Always include ?dataset= URL, e.g. https://habon-ifcb.whoi.edu/timeline?dataset=tangosund

text: db_folder/annotations.sqlite single SQLite database file containing annotations samples. default storage backend: Python dependency required Fast read/write performance Single file samples — easy back manage Contains annotations table (one row per ROI) class_lists table (preserves class indices .mat export) database stored separate Database Folder (configurable Settings), defaults local user-level directory (tools::R_user_dir("ClassiPyR", "data")). separation ensures SQLite database stays local filesystem even Output Folder network drive. title: User Guide
text: format selector, two buttons allow bulk conversion formats: Import .mat → SQLite: Imports .mat annotation files output folder SQLite database. Already-imported samples skipped. Import PNG → SQLite: Imports annotations folder PNG images organized class-name subfolders. Class names extracted folder names (trailing _NNN suffixes stripped). Useful re-importing corrected exports importing external classification datasets. Export SQLite → .mat: Exports annotated samples database .mat files specified Output Folder. Requires Python scipy. Export SQLite → PNG: Extracts annotated images ROI files class-name subfolders PNG Output Folder. Useful building training datasets CNN classifiers. Export SQLite → ZIP: Builds EcoTaxa-ready ZIP archive SQLite annotations also suitable sharing datasets general repositories (example Zenodo Figshare). export writes class-organized PNGs, per-class inventories (ecotaxa_<CLASSNAME>.tsv), README file. Export SQLite → MATLAB ZIP: Builds MATLAB-format ZIP archive via iRfcb::ifcb_zip_matlab(), bundling .mat annotation files, feature CSVs, class2use.mat config file, optional raw data (.roi, .adc, .hdr), README files. using SQLite-storage annotations automatically converted temporary .mat files (requires Python scipy). See iRfcb image export tutorial details MATLAB ZIP format. Export SQLite → ZIP Export SQLite → MATLAB ZIP dialogs include optional README metadata fields: Author Contact e-mail DOI Licence Version Citation Institute values saved settings reused future ZIP exports. Empty fields omitted README. ZIP export also supports archive splitting via split_zip max_size (MB), matching iRfcb::ifcb_zip_pngs(). MATLAB ZIP dialog additionally requires Features folder (top-level folder containing feature CSV files) optional Data folder (raw IFCB data). “search recursively” checkbox enabled default files year-based subdirectories included automatically. ZIP export dialogs include optional Filter IFCB dropdown (shown database contains samples one instrument). lets restrict export single instrument, e.g. exclude data test development instrument. Note: External datasets can viewed without ROI files extracted PNGs available sample-named folders ROI/PNG Data Folder. MAT export still requires ADC data (local files IFCB Dashboard).

text: ClassiPyR created provide lightweight, standalone annotation validation tool fully compatible ifcb-analysis toolbox custom classifiers (e.g. CNN). primary design goals user-friendliness portability—enabling researchers work IFCB data without complex setup requirements dependencies specific computing environments (Python R). achieve goals efficiently, Claude Code used development.

text: roi_folder Path ROI/PNG data folder. NULL, read saved settings. csv_folder Path classification folder (CSV/H5/MAT). NULL, read saved settings. output_folder Path output folder MAT annotations. TRUE data_source = "dashboard", skip scanning local csv_folder classifier output files (dashboard's auto-classifications used instead). title: ClassiPyR (development version)
text: Export SQLite → MATLAB ZIP: New “SQLite → MATLAB ZIP” button Settings bundles .mat annotation files, feature CSVs, class2use.mat config file, optional raw data, READMEs distributable MATLAB-format ZIP archive via iRfcb::ifcb_zip_matlab(). using SQLite-storage, annotations automatically converted .mat files (requires Python scipy). Supports README metadata fields existing PNG ZIP export. IFCB instrument filter ZIP exports: ZIP MATLAB ZIP export dialogs now include “Filter IFCB” dropdown database contains samples multiple instruments. Select one instruments include; deselect instruments exclude archive. Local classifier files dashboard mode: “Use dashboard auto-classifications” disabled Classification Folder configured, local classifier output files (CSV/H5/MAT) now scanned dashboard sync. Classified samples show correct status (checkmark) sample dropdown, consistent loading behavior. Clear Annotations: Added “Clear Annotations” button sample mode permanently deletes sample’s annotations SQLite database (removes .mat file present). button disabled outside annotation mode shows confirmation dialog proceeding. clearing, sample resets blank unclassified state. New exported function delete_annotations_db() programmatic deletion sample’s annotations database.

title: ClassiPyR (development version)
text: Reorganised Settings panel: Folder paths now grouped clear “Input Folders” “Output” sections. ROI/PNG data folder listed first primary input. Database folder annotation storage format grouped together Output. former standalone “Annotation Storage” section merged Output. Added descriptive help text folder path fields Settings. Help text now visually closer field describes, spacing next field. Predict button now always visible (greyed disabled).

title: ClassiPyR (development version)
text: Dashboard auto-classification URL: Fixed class_scores CSV download failing dashboard URLs without ?dataset= parameter. IFCB Dashboard serves class_scores files dataset-specific path (e.g., /mvco/) rather generic /data/ endpoint used file types. new resolve_sample_dataset() function automatically queries Dashboard bin API determine correct dataset explicitly provided URL. Mode switching dashboard mode: Fixed “ROI dimensions available create annotations” error switching validation annotation mode dashboard-loaded samples. mode-switching code now checks dashboard PNG cache ROI dimension inference, just local PNG paths. Python environment detection: Fixed py_discover_config() returning system Python instead active virtualenv Shiny runtime, causing iRfcb’s scipy check fail .mat file operations (class list download, SQLite → .mat export, MATLAB ZIP export). fix sets RETICULATE_PYTHON Python initialization py_discover_config() resolves correct virtualenv binary. Dashboard auto-classification fallback: “Use dashboard auto-classifications” enabled dashboard classifier output sample, user-friendly notification now shown instead logging console warning. Local classifier files longer loaded unintended fallback mode. validation/annotation mode toggle now appears whenever auto-classification data exists sample, manual annotations auto-classifications pre-exist. allows switching annotation mode samples auto-classifications (✓), creating blank annotations fly. Previously samples toggle locked validation mode. session cache now stores restores mode toggle state, toggle longer disappears switching cached samples. Fixed “argument length zero” warning closing app, caused autosave--close handler failing read annotator name class list session already ended.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: Auto-persist class list SQLite: using SQLite storage (default), class list now automatically saved global_class_list table whenever changes — adding classes, renaming, applying WoRMS matches, uploading file, importing PNGs. next startup class list restored database, manual file upload needed. File-based class lists (.mat/.txt) remain supported fallback sharing. New exported functions: save_global_class_list_db() load_global_class_list_db() programmatic access persisted class list.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: Added Import PNG → SQLite button Settings > Import / Export (#15). Imports annotations folder PNG images organized class-name subfolders (e.g. exported ClassiPyR tools). Folder names follow iRfcb convention trailing _NNN suffixes stripped. importing PNG folders class names current class list, class mapping dialog lets users remap unmatched classes existing ones add new classes. Overwrite warning dialog shown imported samples already exist database. New exported functions: scan_png_class_folder() scanning PNG class folder structures, import_png_folder_to_db() programmatic bulk import. Skip class PNG export: New option Settings exclude specific class (e.g. “unclassified”) PNG output. Export SQLite → ZIP (EcoTaxa-ready): Added new export action Settings > Import / Export create ZIP archive directly SQLite annotations. flow exports class-organized PNGs, writes per-class inventories ecotaxa_<CLASSNAME>.tsv (EcoTaxa type row), zips include_txt = TRUE (#21). Customizable ZIP README metadata: ZIP export dialog now supports optional README fields (Author, Contact e-mail, DOI, Licence, Version, Citation, Institute), persisted settings. Empty optional fields omitted generated README. README also appends archive provenance including current ClassiPyR version citation. Optional ZIP splitting: ZIP export dialog now exposes split_zip max_size controls mapped iRfcb::ifcb_zip_pngs(). max_size shown split mode enabled. Local sample discovery now supports extracted PNG sample-folder layouts addition ROI files. simple hierarchical folder structures detected, long sample directories follow IFCB sample naming. Local sample loading now falls back PNG sample folders ROI files unavailable, enabling image viewing annotation workflows PNG-datasets. PNG-samples now derive ROI-like dimensions PNG headers sort images area (width * height) match ROI-based ordering behavior. Folder sync progress now reports staged updates (including long directory scans) “Syncing folders…” progress bar shows meaningful movement instead remaining static. Added safeguards ensure user-managed PNG source folders never deleted temporary-folder cleanup logic.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: HDF5 classification support: Load classifications .h5 files produced iRfcb (>= 0.8.0) (#14). Requires optional hdf5r package. Classification threshold toggle: New “Apply classification threshold” checkbox Settings controls whether thresholded raw predictions used, classification formats (CSV, H5, MAT). Live Prediction: Added “Predict” button Sample Mode classifies images loaded sample using remote CNN model via iRfcb::ifcb_classify_images() (#17). Configure Gradio API URL model Settings > Live Prediction. model dropdown populated dynamically Gradio server. Predictions respect classification threshold setting, skip manually reclassified images, new class names model added class list automatically. per-image progress bar shows classification progress. Class Review Mode: View reclassify annotated images specific class across entire database (#16). Switch class review via mode toggle sidebar, select class, load matching images samples . Changes saved row-level updates database. External PNG folder review: Class Review mode now supports External PNG Folder source addition database. Load folder PNG images, relabel gallery, export class-name subfolders. Useful sorting correcting flat folder images external classifier without importing database. New exported functions: list_classes_db(), load_class_annotations_db(), save_class_review_changes_db() programmatic class review operations.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: WoRMS class matching: Added Match WoRMS AphiaID action Class List Editor. Class names can now matched WoRMS (worrms::wm_records_names()), accepted AphiaIDs stored per class shown inline class list. Matching results distinguish accepted names, synonyms, unmatched classes, skipped long queries (>80 chars). results dialog supports manual rematch editing query text per unmatched class applying. New exported functions: sanitize_worms_query(), build_worms_match_rows(), save_class_taxonomy_db(), load_class_taxonomy_db() programmatic WoRMS/taxonomy workflows.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: IFCB Dashboard support: Connect directly remote IFCB Dashboard instances (e.g. https://habon-ifcb.whoi.edu/) without downloading data locally (#13). Toggle “Local Folders” “IFCB Dashboard” Settings, enter Dashboard URL (optional ?dataset= parameter), browse samples API. Images downloaded demand cached locally. Optionally load dashboard auto-classifications validation mode. Supports MAT export downloading ADC files demand, graceful fallback SQLite-ADC unavailable. Configurable dashboard download settings: Dashboard mode now exposes parallel downloads, sleep time, timeout, max retries “Advanced Download Settings” section Settings. Local classification files dashboard mode: Classification Folder setting now available dashboard mode. configured, local CSV/H5/MAT classification files take priority dashboard auto-classifications, dashboard autoclass fallback. New exported functions: parse_dashboard_url(), list_dashboard_bins(), download_dashboard_images(), download_dashboard_images_bulk(), download_dashboard_image_single(), download_dashboard_images_individual(), download_dashboard_adc(), download_dashboard_autoclass(), get_dashboard_cache_dir() programmatic dashboard access.

title: ClassiPyR 0.2.0
text: class list editor now shows number annotated images per class parentheses, queried SQLite database. class list editor now optionally shows [AphiaID: ...] per class WoRMS mappings exist. Added Apply button Settings activate changes immediately without closing dialog (#18). Prevented shinyFiles freezing invalid path entered disabling Browse showing notification non-existent input folders (#19). Select Page / Select : “Select ” button now uses two-click workflow — first click selects current page, second click selects images across pages. Resets automatically navigating pages, changing filters, loading new sample. annotation mode, images now sorted width (x-dimension, descending) instead area, classes. groups images similar widths uniform gallery layout. Gallery images native resolution: Removed max-height: 120px CSS constraint images display actual pixel dimensions. preserves small morphological details ensures measuring tool reports accurate distances.
